channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Internet Archive is back online after a cyberattack
item.description: The Internet Archive is back online after new of a cyberattack took out its digital library and popular Wayback Machine on October 9.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Nuclear Power Partnership: Google Agrees to Buy 500 Megawatts from Small Modular Reactors
item.description: Google's historic agreement with Kairos Power aims to meet electricity demand for artificial intelligence, marking a significant step in the tech giant's efforts to reduce its carbon footprint.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: The Global Space Race Heats Up: NASA, China, Europe, and SpaceX Compete in Earth's Orbit and on the Moon
item.description: The world's space agencies are meeting in Milan as geopolitical rivalry fuels a new global race in Earth's orbit and on the moon. Private companies are heavily involved, with SpaceX leading the way.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Bankinter and Microsoft Partner to Revolutionize Work with AI-Powered Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Bankinter, in partnership with Microsoft, has been implementing Microsoft 365 Copilot in various areas of the bank for about a year. The goal is to test the generative AI solution from Redmond in internal tasks related to data extraction and processing.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows Users Targeted by Sophisticated Malware: What You Need to Know
item.description: Cybersecurity experts warn of a new type of malware spreading through fake human verification pages on Windows devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murder trial of East Bay tech consultant in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee begins
item.description: Lee’s death stunned the tech community, and fellow executives and engineers penned tributes to his generosity and brilliance. Lee was chief product officer of cryptocurrency platform MobileCoin when he died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk unveils long-awaited robotaxi
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk unveiled the electric vehicle (EV) company’s long-awaited robotaxi Thursday night, showing off a fleet of sleek self-driving cars with no steering wheels or pedals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Login.gov facial recognition option gets GSA approval
item.description: Login.gov will allow its users to match a selfie with the photo on a government ID, such as a driver’s license. The post Login.gov facial recognition option gets GSA approval first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Deno 2.0 arrives, ready to battle Node.js
item.description: Deno 2.0, a major update to the open source Deno runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, is now available as a production release. It emphasizes backward compatibility with the rival Node.js runtime and NPM and a stabilized standard library.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11
